g.title: Each Rotor.run_*() method returns a results object with numerical data and visualization methods (interactive plots via Plotly).
All ROSS plots use Plotly. Interactive plots: zoom, pan, hover, PNG/SVG/HTML export. Works in Jupyter Notebooks and scripts.
Result of run_static() Data: deflection, shear, moment, reactions Plots: deformation, shearing_force, bending_moment, free_body_diagram
Result of run_modal() Data: wn (natural freq.), wd (damped), log_dec (logarithmic decrement), damping_ratio, lam (eigenvalues) Plots: mode_3d, mode_2d, orbit
Result of run_campbell() Data: frequencies vs. speed, critical speeds, whirl direction (FW/BW) Plots: campbell diagram, with_mode_shape
Result of run_freq_response() Data: magnitude, phase, complex FRF Plots: magnitude, phase, polar_bode
Result of run_unbalance_response() and run_forced_response() Data: magnitude, phase per node and speed Plots: magnitude, phase, bode, deflected_shape_2d, deflected_shape_3d
Result of run_time_response(), run_rubbing(), run_crack(), run_misalignment() Data: t (time), yout (displacements), xout (states) Plots: 1d, 2d (orbit), 3d, dfft
Result of run_ucs() Data: critical speeds vs. stiffness Plot: log-log map with bearing reference
Result of run_level1() Data: Q0 (threshold), Qa (applied) Plot: Q0 vs. Qa comparison (API 617)
Result of run_harmonic_balance_response() Data: harmonic amplitudes, convergence Plot: nonlinear frequency response
Saves results to file. Format: pickle (.pkl) or JSON. Allows re-analysis without re-running.
Loads previously saved results. Class method (classmethod). Ex: results = ModalResults.load("file.pkl")
Zoom, pan, hover with values Curve selection (legend toggle) Comparison of multiple results
PNG, SVG for publications Interactive HTML for web reports Plotly JSON for reuse
Inline rendering in notebooks Interactive widgets for parameter and mode exploration
Each Rotor.run_*() method returns a results object with numerical data and visualization methods (interactive plots via Plotly).
All ROSS plots use Plotly. Interactive plots: zoom, pan, hover, PNG/SVG/HTML export. Works in Jupyter Notebooks and scripts.
Result of run_static() Data: deflection, shear, moment, reactions Plots: deformation, shearing_force, bending_moment, free_body_diagram
Result of run_modal() Data: wn (natural freq.), wd (damped), log_dec (logarithmic decrement), damping_ratio, lam (eigenvalues) Plots: mode_3d, mode_2d, orbit
Result of run_campbell() Data: frequencies vs. speed, critical speeds, whirl direction (FW/BW) Plots: campbell diagram, with_mode_shape
Result of run_freq_response() Data: magnitude, phase, complex FRF Plots: magnitude, phase, polar_bode
Result of run_unbalance_response() and run_forced_response() Data: magnitude, phase per node and speed Plots: magnitude, phase, bode, deflected_shape_2d, deflected_shape_3d
Result of run_time_response(), run_rubbing(), run_crack(), run_misalignment() Data: t (time), yout (displacements), xout (states) Plots: 1d, 2d (orbit), 3d, dfft
Result of run_ucs() Data: critical speeds vs. stiffness Plot: log-log map with bearing reference
Result of run_level1() Data: Q0 (threshold), Qa (applied) Plot: Q0 vs. Qa comparison (API 617)
Result of run_harmonic_balance_response() Data: harmonic amplitudes, convergence Plot: nonlinear frequency response
Saves results to file. Format: pickle (.pkl) or JSON. Allows re-analysis without re-running.
Loads previously saved results. Class method (classmethod). Ex: results = ModalResults.load("file.pkl")
Zoom, pan, hover with values Curve selection (legend toggle) Comparison of multiple results
PNG, SVG for publications Interactive HTML for web reports Plotly JSON for reuse
Inline rendering in notebooks Interactive widgets for parameter and mode exploration
